title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of AECOM - ACM
author: Pomerantz Llp
description: Pomerantz LLP is investigating AECOM for potential securities fraud following the company's disclosure of significantly delayed claim resolutions on projects from 2019-2020. AECOM reported a 98% decline in quarterly operating cash flow to $4 million and negative adjusted free cash flow of $27 million in Q2 fiscal 2026. The company's claims recorded in contract assets surged from $400 million to $680 million, leading to a 12% stock price decline of $9.55 per share on May 12, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: AECOM faces a securities fraud investigation due to undisclosed delays in claim resolutions, resulting in severe operational deterioration (98% cash flow decline), massive increase in outstanding claims ($280 million increase), and significant shareholder losses (12% stock decline). The delayed disclosure of material information suggests potential misconduct.

title: AECOM Shareholders Are Encouraged to Reach Out to Johnson Fistel for More Information about Potentially Recovering Their Losses
author: Johnson Fistel, Pllp
description: Law firm Johnson Fistel is investigating whether AECOM violated federal securities laws following the company's May 2026 earnings report, which revealed a 98% year-over-year decline in operating cash flow to $4 million, negative free cash flow of $27 million, and a significant increase in claims recorded in contract assets from $400 million to $680 million. The investigation seeks to determine if investors can recover losses related to these disclosures.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company reported a dramatic 98% decline in operating cash flow, negative free cash flow, and a significant increase in unresolved contract claims ($680 million vs. $400 million), which triggered a federal securities investigation for potential violations of securities laws and investor losses.

title: AECOM JV Wins Key Role In Singapore's Next-Gen Waste Megaproject
author: Akanksha Bakshi
description: AECOM's joint venture with Binnies and Ramboll was appointed by Singapore's National Environment Agency to deliver consultancy services for Phase 2 of the Integrated Waste Management Facility at Tuas Nexus. The stock was up 0.84% at $71.78 on Wednesday. Despite the positive contract win, AECOM shares remain down 33.72% over the past year and are trading below key moving averages, with technical indicators showing oversold conditions. Analysts maintain a Buy rating with a $110 price target.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed signals: positive news from winning a significant Singapore waste management contract and analyst Buy rating with $110 price target, but offset by weak technical position (down 33.72% YTM, trading below moving averages, weak value and growth scores). The stock showed modest gains (+0.84%) on the news but remains fundamentally challenged.

title: Nasdaq 100 Dips On Hot CPI, Sandisk And Micron Tumble 9%: Stock Market Today
author: Piero Cingari
description: U.S. equities fell sharply on May 12, 2026, after April's CPI came in hotter than expected at 3.8%, raising concerns the Federal Reserve will keep rates on hold throughout the year. The Nasdaq 100 sank 2.1% as the semiconductor sector collapsed, with memory chip stocks hit particularly hard by South Korea's proposed 'AI Citizen Dividend.' Notable gainers included Zebra Technologies (+18%) and Wendy's (+15%), while ZoomInfo plummeted 33% on slashed guidance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock tumbled 9.0% due to broad-based semiconductor sector collapse triggered by hot CPI data and South Korea's proposed AI dividend threatening memory chip gains.
Dropped 8.6% as memory chip stocks were hit hard by concerns over South Korea's 'AI Citizen Dividend' and broader semiconductor weakness.

title: 3 Energy Stocks to Buy and 2 to Avoid as AI Power Demand Explodes
author: Bridget Bennett
description: As AI data centers require massive uninterrupted power, energy infrastructure becomes the critical bottleneck. The article identifies three energy stocks positioned to benefit from 'bring your own power' solutions that bypass grid constraints: GE Vernova (turbine manufacturing with strong backlog), Bloom Energy (fuel cell technology), and Kodiak Gas Services (distributed power generation). Two stocks to avoid are NextEra Energy (misaligned with baseload power needs) and AECOM (lacks relevant project exposure).

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong order backlog of 83 GW with turbine reservations sold out through 2030. Margin expansion potential as legacy costs burn off and capacity increases. Analyst sees room for further gains despite significant recent run.

Secured major Oracle Project Jupiter contract for 2.45-2.8 GW of fuel cell capacity. Clear path to scale from 100 MW to 5 GW annually by 2030. Recurring revenue model from catalyst replenishment provides long-term earnings visibility.

Acquired Distributed Power Solutions adding 395 MW of generation capacity with two-thirds already contracted to data centers. Positioned for 'double dip' benefit from increased natural gas demand and premium data center power pricing.

Structurally misaligned with AI data center needs. Wind and solar cannot provide required baseload power; battery backup insufficient for overnight operations. Data centers need consistent 24/7 power regardless of weather.
